title: Centaurium compressum
taxonomy: Centaurium compressum is a species of flowering plant, and it belongs to the family Gentianaceae. This family is commonly known as the gentian family. The genus Centaurium itself is a group of plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere, with species found in Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The classification of Centaurium compressum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Gentianales. It is a part of the angiosperm division of flowering plants.
uses: Centaurium compressum is known to be used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory properties. It is often used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Some studies suggest that the plant may have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which could contribute to its medicinal uses. The plant is traditionally used in the form of a tea or infusion to harness its health benefits. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

constituents: Centaurium compressum is known to contain various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. These compounds are believed to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The specific flavonoids found in Centaurium compressum include quercetin, kaempferol, and myricetin. parts: Centaurium compressum is a plant species commonly known as the smallow or narrow-leaved centaury, but information regarding its medicinal parts is not readily available.
preparations: Centaurium compressum is not typically associated with well-documented herbal preparations, but it may be used by herbalists or practitioners in specific applications due to its potential properties. intro: Centaurium compressum, also known as goldthread, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible tubers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Centaurium compressum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Goldthread is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are commonly used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
